CONTEXT:
Intent: Product: TinyURL is a frictionless, public URL shortening service ensuring links are instantly accessible and reliably tracked without requiring user accounts or complex setup. It empowers Link Sharers, Clickers, and API Clients through a secure, minimal architecture focused on speed and simplicity. — TinyURL is a minimal HTTP service that converts long URLs into short, six-character slugs for public sharing and instantly redirects traffic back to the original destination. It operates without user authentication, focusing purely on the core value of link compression and reliable delivery. The system leverages domains like the URL Shortening Engine, Threat Detection Gateway, and Encrypted Storage Layer to ensure security and performance, while integrations manage rate limiting, disaster recovery, and logging.
Who it serves: Link Sharer — Any user needing to share a long URL but wants to minimize space and clutter. Does not require login or persistence.
Problem: TinyURL is a minimal HTTP service that converts long URLs into short, six-character slugs for public sharing and instantly redirects traffic back to the original destination. It operates without user authentication, focusing purely on the core value of link compression and reliable delivery. The system leverages domains like the URL Shortening Engine, Threat Detection Gateway, and Encrypted Storage Layer to ensure security and performance, while integrations manage rate limiting, disaster recovery, and logging.

[Release 2] NFR-AVAIL-002-D-001 [high] (Tier D leaf under NFR-002): As a system, I want to verify monthly uptime for redirect endpoint including planned maintenance is verified via synthetic probes, so that Verified uptime ≥ 99.9% over rolling 30-day windows; maintenance windows excluded per policy.
Non-Functional Requirements: 15 NFRs:
  NFR-001 [performance]: Redirect endpoint latency P95 under nominal load — threshold: P95 latency for the redirect endpoint must be ≤ 100 ms over any rolling 5-minute window
  NFR-002 [availability]: Monthly uptime for redirect endpoint including planned maintenance — threshold: Monthly uptime ≥ 99.9% over rolling 30-day windows, inclusive of planned maintenance windows.
  NFR-003 [availability]: Recovery Time Objective on full regional failure — threshold: Recovery Time Objective (RTO) on full regional failure is ≤ 15 minutes.
  NFR-004 [security]: Original URLs stored encrypted at rest using AES-256 — threshold: Zero plaintext occurrence of any submitted URL in direct read of underlying Postgres data files; AES-256 cipher suite mandatory for all stored payload columns.
  NFR-005 [observability]: Structured JSON logs to stdout ingested by platform — threshold: All logs conform to structured JSON and stream to stdout; audit logs retained for ≥ 365 days; ingestion failure alerts triggered within < 1 minute.
  NFR-006 [security]: HTTPS enforced on all public endpoints with HSTS headers — threshold: HTTP requests must redirect to HTTPS; HTTPS only on all public endpoints; HSTS headers enabled; SSL/TLS 1.2 or higher required.
  NFR-007 [reliability]: Every stored slug in the mappings table must be unique — threshold: SELECT slug, COUNT(*) FROM url_mappings GROUP BY slug HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 returns zero rows
  NFR-008 [compliance]: Deletion requests from Sharers purged from storage and cache — threshold: 100% of valid deletion requests from Sharers result in full purging from persistent storage and ephemeral cache within 24 hours of receipt; zero retention beyond this window.
  NFR-009 [compliance]: GDPR data export for VOC-SHORT-URLs under Right to Access — threshold: Export file contains 100% of stored link data associated with the requesting user; zero data loss or omission allowed.
  NFR-010 [reliability]: Daily automated backup execution and backup replication integrity — threshold: 100% of daily scheduled backup jobs execute successfully; zero replication integrity errors detected.
  NFR-011 [security]: Threat feed blocking latency and IP reputation scoring — threshold: Threat indicator blocking latency ≤ 5 seconds for all valid requests; IP reputation scoring accuracy ≥ 95% on validation queries; Threat feed update frequency ≤ 15 minutes.
  NFR-012 [scalability]: Rate limit enforced at 1000 requests per minute per IP — threshold: 1000 requests per IP address per rolling 60-second window; excess requests rejected with HTTP 429 status code.
  NFR-013 [security]: Malicious URL submission blocked before storage — threshold: Zero malicious URL submissions successfully stored in the content repository.
  NFR-014 [compliance]: Notification when VOC-POLICY-RULE dictates data expiry — threshold: Notification attempt generated for every policy-expiry event before the expiry timestamp; zero missed notifications for active retention records.
  NFR-015 [reliability]: Handling timeout condition without service crash — threshold: Zero service crashes triggered by timeout conditions.
